skill.name: content-research-writer
skill.description: Guides research-backed content creation from outline to final draft, with support for citations, hooks, section feedback, and voice-preserving revisions.
skill.summary: Plan, research, draft, cite, and polish long-form content with structured writing feedback.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Ruby backtick execution patterns and three weak cryptography patterns, but review found no Ruby code or cryptographic implementation. The only actual shell commands are local setup examples using mkdir, cd, and touch, so the remaining risk is limited to user-run filesystem changes described in documentation.

low_findings.title: Local Shell Setup Commands in Documentation
low_findings.description: The setup instructions show mkdir, cd, and touch commands for creating a writing workspace. These are not executed by the skill automatically and do not include user-controlled command interpolation, network access, credential access, or destructive operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in fenced documentation examples and are limited to creating a directory and draft file. They present minor filesystem risk only if a user manually runs them in an unintended location.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Are Mostly Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The numerous Ruby backtick execution findings point to fenced markdown blocks and example content rather than executable Ruby. The weak cryptography findings at the cited lines are prose false positives, with no evidence of cryptographic code or insecure algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed the cited locations are markdown templates, sample responses, or ordinary prose. No evidence found of executable Ruby, backtick command substitution, or weak cryptographic algorithm use.

content.value_statement: Long-form content is hard to plan, source, and revise consistently. This skill guides outlines, research notes, citations, section feedback, and final polishing in one workflow.
actual_capabilities: Builds article, newsletter, tutorial, and thought leadership outlines from a topic and audience.
Identifies research gaps and suggests where citations, examples, or data should support claims.
Improves introductions with alternative hooks based on story, data, question, or bold claim formats.
Reviews draft sections for clarity, flow, evidence, tone, and specific line edits.
Maintains running citation lists in inline, numbered reference, or footnote formats.
Provides final draft reviews with structure, readability, citation, and pre-publish checks.
limitations: It depends on available tools for live research and cannot verify sources without access to them.
It may suggest example claims that still need source verification before publication.
It does not automatically publish content or manage external content management systems.
It needs writing samples or explicit style guidance to preserve a specific author voice.

use_cases.title: Plan a Research-Backed Blog Post
use_cases.description: Turn a topic into a structured outline with research tasks, source needs, and section goals.

use_cases.title: Improve a Draft One Section at a Time
use_cases.description: Get focused feedback on clarity, flow, evidence, and tone as each section is written.

use_cases.title: Prepare Technical Tutorials
use_cases.description: Organize steps, explanations, examples, troubleshooting notes, and source references before final review.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Starter Outline
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a new article.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create an outline for an article about [topic]. The audience is [audience], and the goal is [goal]. Include research gaps and citation needs.

prompt_templates.title: Research a Specific Section
prompt_templates.scenario: Add evidence before drafting a section.
prompt_templates.prompt: Research [specific topic] for my [section name] section. Summarize key facts, note source quality, and suggest where each citation should appear.

prompt_templates.title: Improve My Introduction
prompt_templates.prompt: Here is my introduction: [paste text]. Analyze the hook, explain what works, and give three stronger alternatives in different styles.

prompt_templates.title: Perform a Full Draft Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this full draft for structure, argument strength, source coverage, voice consistency, readability, and publication readiness: [paste draft]. Provide prioritized edits.

output_examples.input: I need an outline for a newsletter about AI tools for product managers.
output_examples.output: A structured newsletter outline with a hook, thesis, three main sections, research tasks, citation needs, and a concise closing call to action.

output_examples.input: Review my section on common product discovery mistakes.
output_examples.output: Section feedback covering strengths, unclear claims, missing evidence, suggested reordering, and specific sentence-level improvements.

output_examples.input: Help me add citations to my draft about remote work productivity.
output_examples.output: A source plan that maps claims to needed evidence, proposes citation placement, and creates a references section format.

best_practices: Provide the audience, goal, target length, and preferred tone before asking for an outline.
Verify every sourced claim against original publications before publishing the final draft.
Share writing samples when voice preservation matters.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated statistics or quotes without checking the original source.
Do not ask for a full rewrite when you want to preserve a personal voice.
Do not mix unrelated audiences or goals in one draft request.

faq.question: Can this skill write a complete article?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide outlining, drafting, research placement, revision, and final review when you provide topic and audience details.

faq.question: Does it find live sources automatically?
faq.answer: Only when the host tool has research or browsing access. Otherwise, it can create a source plan and citation checklist.

faq.question: Can it preserve my writing style?
faq.answer: Yes, when you provide samples or clear tone preferences. It is designed to suggest improvements without replacing your voice.

faq.question: What citation formats does it support?
faq.answer: It supports inline citations, numbered references, and footnotes. You can request another format if needed.

faq.question: Is it useful for technical documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline tutorials, explain steps, add troubleshooting sections, and review accuracy and clarity.

faq.question: What should I check before publishing?
faq.answer: Verify sources, confirm quotes, check factual claims, proofread final text, and ensure the draft matches the intended audience.
